meta.title: Gold Price & Safe Haven Dashboard | Fear Gauge Live — SafeHaven Dash
meta.description: Track gold, USD index, CHF, JPY in real-time. Free safe-haven dashboard with live Fear Gauge. See if markets are calm or panicking.

fearGauge.title: Market Fear Level
fearGauge.subtitle: Based on gold, USD, CHF, JPY movements
fearGauge.methodologyDesc: The SafeHaven Fear Gauge combines 24-hour percentage changes in four safe-haven indicators with the following weights:
fearGauge.dxyDesc: USD Index (DXY): 25% — rising dollar = flight to safety
fearGauge.chfDesc: CHF strength: 20% — Swiss franc strengthening = more fear
fearGauge.jpyDesc: JPY strength: 20% — Japanese yen strengthening = more fear
fearGauge.scoreRange: The composite score is normalized to a 0-100 scale: 0-30 (Calm), 31-60 (Cautious), 61-100 (Fear).

calculator.title: Calculate Your Fear Score
calculator.subtitle: Select the safe-haven assets you currently hold to see your personalized fear exposure.
calculator.privacyNote: We collect anonymous usage data to improve the service. No personal information is stored.
calculator.errorSelectOne: Please select at least one asset.
calculator.errorAlreadySubmitted: You have already submitted this session.
calculator.successMessage: Your fear score has been recorded!
calculator.errorGeneric: Something went wrong. Please try again.

methodology.title: How the Fear Gauge Works
methodology.intro: The SafeHaven Fear Gauge is a proprietary composite index that measures market anxiety by tracking four key safe-haven indicators in real-time.
methodology.goldDetail: Gold (XAU/USD) — 35%: Gold is the classic safe-haven asset. When gold prices rise sharply, it typically signals increased market fear and uncertainty.
methodology.dxyDetail: USD Index (DXY) — 25%: The US Dollar Index measures the dollar against a basket of major currencies. A strengthening dollar often indicates flight to safety.
methodology.chfDetail: Swiss Franc (CHF) — 20%: The Swiss franc is considered one of the world's safest currencies. CHF strengthening against USD signals risk aversion.
methodology.jpyDetail: Japanese Yen (JPY) — 20%: The yen traditionally strengthens during market stress as carry trades unwind and investors seek safety.
methodology.disclaimer: Disclaimer: The Fear Gauge is for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.

footer.disclaimer: Data is provided for informational purposes only and should not be considered financial advice. Prices may be delayed. Always do your own research before making investment decisions.

about.title: About SafeHaven Dash
about.whatIsP1: SafeHaven Dash is a free, real-time financial dashboard designed to give retail investors, macro-economy watchers, and FX traders a single, clean view of the world's most important safe-haven assets. We track Gold (XAU/USD), the US Dollar Index (DXY), Swiss Franc (USD/CHF), and Japanese Yen (USD/JPY) — the four pillars of safety during market turbulence.
about.whatIsP2: At the heart of our dashboard sits the Fear Gauge, a proprietary composite index that distills these four asset movements into a single number from 0 to 100, answering the question every investor asks during a crisis: "How scared is the market right now?"
about.problemP1: During market stress — geopolitical crises, central bank surprises, inflation shocks — investors scramble across multiple platforms to check gold prices, currency pairs, and risk sentiment indicators. Existing tools are often cluttered with ads, locked behind paywalls, or scatter safe-haven data across dozens of pages.
about.problemP2: SafeHaven Dash consolidates everything into a single-page, mobile-friendly dashboard that loads in under 2 seconds, auto-refreshes every 60 seconds, and costs absolutely nothing to use. No registration. No paywall. No clutter.
about.whoTraders: Retail FX & Commodity Traders — Monitor gold and safe-haven currencies in one view before placing trades.
about.whoMacro: Macro-Economy Enthusiasts — Stay informed about risk sentiment and capital flows without subscribing to Bloomberg or Reuters.
about.whoBloggers: Financial Bloggers & Analysts — Reference our Fear Gauge in your content as a real-time sentiment snapshot.
about.whoEveryday: Everyday Investors — Anyone searching "gold price today" or "is the market crashing?" deserves a clear, instant answer.
about.howP1: Our Fear Gauge is a weighted composite index computed on our servers every 5 minutes. It combines the 24-hour percentage change of four safe-haven indicators:
about.howGold: Gold (XAU/USD) — 35% weight: Rising gold is the classic flight-to-safety signal.
about.howDxy: US Dollar Index (DXY) — 25% weight: A strengthening dollar indicates capital flight to the world's reserve currency.
about.howChf: Swiss Franc (CHF) — 20% weight: CHF strengthening reflects European risk aversion.
about.howJpy: Japanese Yen (JPY) — 20% weight: JPY appreciation signals carry-trade unwinding and global deleveraging.
about.howP2: The raw weighted sum is normalized to a 0-100 scale: 0-30 (Calm), 31-60 (Cautious), 61-100 (Fear). The result is displayed as an animated gauge with real-time updates.
about.techP1: SafeHaven Dash is built with Next.js for server-side rendering and SEO optimization, Tailwind CSS for a responsive mobile-first design, and Recharts for interactive sparkline charts. We aggregate data from multiple free API providers with server-side caching and intelligent fallback chains to ensure 99.9% uptime at zero infrastructure cost.
about.techP2: The entire project is open-source and deployed on Vercel's free tier, proving that powerful financial tools don't have to be expensive.
about.contactP1: Have feedback, suggestions, or partnership inquiries? We'd love to hear from you. Reach us at

howToUse.title: How to Use SafeHaven Dash
howToUse.intro: SafeHaven Dash is designed to be intuitive — no registration, no setup, no learning curve. Here's how to get the most out of it in three simple steps.
howToUse.step1P1: The moment you land on SafeHaven Dash, the Fear Gauge at the top of the page shows you the current market fear level (0-100). Green means calm markets, amber signals caution, and red indicates elevated fear. The gauge updates automatically every 60 seconds.
howToUse.step1P2: Below the gauge, you'll see the component breakdown showing how much each asset (Gold, DXY, CHF, JPY) is contributing to the overall score.
howToUse.step2P1: Scroll down to the Live Safe Haven Prices section. Each card displays the current price, 24-hour change (in both absolute and percentage terms), and a 7-day sparkline chart showing the recent trend.
howToUse.step2Gold: Gold (XAU/USD): The spot price of one troy ounce of gold in US dollars.
howToUse.step2Dxy: USD Index (DXY): The dollar's value against a basket of six major currencies.
howToUse.step2Chf: USD/CHF: How many Swiss Francs one US dollar buys. A falling value means CHF is strengthening.
howToUse.step2Jpy: USD/JPY: How many Japanese Yen one US dollar buys. A falling value means JPY is strengthening.
howToUse.step2P2: Green arrows indicate the asset has risen; red arrows indicate a decline. The sparkline gives you a quick visual of the week's momentum.
howToUse.step3P1: Use the interactive widget to see how your portfolio exposure relates to the current market fear. Select the safe-haven assets you currently hold.
howToUse.step3P2: Click 'Calculate & Record' to anonymously record your response. This data helps us improve the dashboard. No personal information is collected.

faq.a: Yes, 100% free with no registration required. We sustain the service through non-intrusive advertising.

faq.q: How often is the data updated?
faq.a: Server-side every 5 minutes, client-side auto-refresh every 60 seconds.
A proprietary composite index combining Gold (35%), DXY (25%), CHF (20%), JPY (20%) changes into a 0-100 score.

faq.q: Can I use this on my phone?
faq.a: Yes. Built mobile-first, works on any device from 320px to 4K. Dark mode fully supported.

faq.q: Why is the DXY available here for free?
faq.a: We compute DXY synthetically from individual currency pairs using the official ICE basket weights.

faq.q: What if data APIs go down?
faq.a: Multi-provider fallback system with 5-minute cache. A 'Delayed' badge appears if data is stale.
No. This is an informational tool only. Always consult a qualified financial advisor.

goldPrice.title: Gold Price Today
goldPrice.subtitle: Live XAU/USD price with 7-day chart, updated every 5 minutes. Track whether gold is signaling market fear or calm.
goldPrice.viewLive: View Live Gold Price on Dashboard
goldPrice.whyTrackP1: Gold (XAU/USD) is the world's most watched safe-haven asset. When markets panic, gold typically rises as investors flee riskier assets.
goldPrice.roleTitle: Gold's Role in the Fear Gauge
goldPrice.roleP1: On SafeHaven Dash, gold carries the highest weight (35%) in our composite Fear Gauge. When gold prices rise sharply, it's often a sign that investors are worried about the economy, geopolitics, or inflation.
goldPrice.readTitle: How to Read Gold Price Charts
goldPrice.read1: Rising gold + rising USD = strong flight to safety
goldPrice.read2: Rising gold + falling USD = inflation hedge / dollar weakness
goldPrice.read3: Falling gold + rising stocks = risk-on sentiment, calm markets
goldPrice.read4: Gold flat = markets are in a wait-and-see mode

faq.q: What is the gold price today?
faq.a: The live gold price is updated every 5 minutes on our dashboard.

faq.q: Why does gold go up during market fear?
faq.a: Gold is a safe-haven asset. Investors buy gold during uncertainty, driving prices up.

faq.q: How often is the gold price updated?
faq.a: Server-side: every 5 minutes. Client-side: auto-refreshes every 60 seconds.

faq.q: Is this real-time gold data free?
faq.a: Yes! 100% free. No sign-up, no paywall required.

fearIndex.title: Market Fear Index Today
fearIndex.subtitle: A real-time composite fear gauge (0-100) that tracks gold, USD, CHF, and JPY to measure market sentiment.
fearIndex.whatIsTitle: What is the SafeHaven Fear Gauge?
fearIndex.whatIsP1: The SafeHaven Fear Gauge is a proprietary composite index that tracks safe-haven asset movements to measure real-time market fear.
fearIndex.calmDesc: Markets are relaxed. Safe-haven demand is low.
fearIndex.fearDesc: Active flight to safety. High uncertainty.

faq.q: How is this different from the VIX?
faq.a: The VIX measures stock option volatility. Our Fear Gauge focuses on safe-haven assets investors actually buy when scared.

faq.q: What does a score of 50 mean?
faq.a: A score of 50 is the baseline — safe-haven assets haven't moved significantly in 24 hours.

faq.q: Can I use this for trading?
faq.a: The Fear Gauge is informational only, not financial advice. Always do your own research.
